title: Fermented Slovakian Goat Dumplings
description: Fluffy dumplings made with ground goat, mashed potatoes, and fermented pickles, seasoned with garlic, ginger, and dill for a festive, nut‑free Slovakian taste.
instructions: Boil the potatoes until tender, then mash them and let cool slightly.
In a large bowl, combine the ground goat meat, mashed potatoes, chopped pickles, minced garlic, minced ginger, dill, salt, and pepper.
Mix the ingredients thoroughly until the mixture becomes cohesive and holds together; add a splash of water if too dry.
Shape the mixture into small dumplings (about 2–3 cm in diameter).
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil; drop the dumplings in and simmer for 12 minutes or until they rise to the surface and are cooked through.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Remove the dumplings from the pot with a slotted spoon and lightly fry in the skillet until golden brown on the outside, about 2–3 minutes per side.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with extra dill and a squeeze of lemon.
Enjoy the festive, fluffy texture and tangy fermented flavors!
